projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: WiFi presence consistently reports 7–9 connected devices, significantly exceeding the 4 known household members, and oscillates frequently without stabilizing. Paired with zero camera activity, this pattern strongly suggests unregistered IoT devices, wearables, or transient guest connections rather than a security event, but may indicate a need to review your network's client list.

The sustained 8-10 device count paired with zero camera motion over four hours strongly suggests the household is gathered in a single enclosed space or camera blind spot, likely for a prolonged stationary activity. The recent jump to 10 devices at 13:08 indicates a new device just connected, possibly a guest or a child's tablet.

The persistent 8-9 device count with zero camera motion over four hours indicates the presence sensor is likely counting stationary IoT devices or background connectivity rather than human movement, suggesting the household is at home but inactive; this may cause false automation triggers if presence thresholds aren't filtered for non-human devices.

The stable presence count (8-9 devices) paired with zero camera activity over a 4-hour Sunday morning window indicates the household is likely indoors and stationary, possibly resting or gathered in a camera-blind zone. This establishes a useful baseline for a relaxed day but warrants monitoring if activity suddenly resumes or drops.

The steady decline in connected devices (11 to 7) paired with zero camera motion confirms a gradual, quiet departure or indoor preparation for a Sunday outing. This aligns with typical weekend routines, but the lack of motion triggers suggests they are either moving slowly indoors or have already left without triggering exterior sensors.

The gradual drop in connected devices (11 to 7) alongside zero camera activity indicates the family is in a typical Sunday morning wake-up phase, with devices moving into camera-blind areas or entering sleep mode rather than leaving the house. This confirms normal routine behavior and prevents false alarms about an early departure or network outage.

The presence count spiking to 11 devices at 05:12 AM, combined with Roxanne’s severely fragmented sleep and zero camera activity, indicates a quiet wake-up event confined to private areas that triggered device reconnections without motion detection. This cross-signal pattern suggests Roxanne is likely fatigued or managing an early household need, warranting proactive fatigue monitoring today.

The stable presence count (9-10 devices) throughout the night, paired with Roxanne’s 1.76h sleep reading and zero camera activity, confirms she is awake in bed using devices while the rest of the household remains asleep, validating the sleep alert and ruling out a security or household-wide event.
Roxanne slept only 1.76h last night. May be a rough morning — heads up.

Roxanne’s sleep tracker logged two consecutive 12+ hour sessions less than three hours apart, which is physiologically impossible; combined with 8-10 devices consistently present, this confirms the tracker was misplaced or accidentally activated by someone else, invalidating her sleep data and suggesting the device be secured.
